data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency only in the regime where the critic achieves high recall on wrong candidates while maintaining precision on right candidates that is bounded below by a function of base error rate and k; specifically, the harness advantage is monotonically governed by the inequality (1 - psi) * phi > psi * (1 - phi) * (k-1)/k, where phi is critic precision on flagged-wrong and psi is critic recall on actually-wrong candidates.","mechanism": "Let B = base error rate of individual candidates. Self-consistency correctness at k=5 is approximately the probability that the correct answer is the modal answer, which increases with k and decreases with B but is non-linear due to correlated errors. Harness filtering removes candidates the critic flags as wrong. Net benefit = (correct candidates removed from wrong-majority questions that flip to right) - (correct candidates removed from right-majority questions that flip to wrong or tie). When B is very low (as in the observed data: 0.01-0.033), most majorities are already correct, so the left term is near zero and even low precision destroys value. When B is moderate (0.15-0.40), wrong majorities exist in sufficient quantity that a critic with psi >= 0.6 and phi >= 0.5 can flip enough of them. The loss condition arises when phi < 0.5: false flags outnumber true flags and erode correct majorities.","scope": {"covers": ["k=5 to k=15", "base error 0.15-0.45", "critic precision 0.4-0.8", "critic recall 0.5-0.9", "uncorrelated candidate errors"], "excludes": ["base error < 0.10 (ceiling effect, no wrong majorities to fix)", "base error > 0.6 (degenerate: majority always wrong, filtering cannot recover signal)", "correlated candidate errors (violates independence assumption)", "k < 3"]},"forbidden": [{"case": "At k=5, base error 0.30, critic precision 0.60, critic recall 0.70, harness accuracy is not strictly greater than self-consistency by at least 0.05", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, base error 0.30, critic precision 0.35, critic recall 0.70, harness accuracy is not strictly less than self-consistency by at least 0.03 (false-flag erosion)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, base error 0.05, critic precision 0.80, critic recall 0.90, harness shows strict improvement over self-consistency > 0.02 (would contradict ceiling-attrition claim)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "The measured critic precision-recall frontier is not stable across two independent hard-set replications within 0.05", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}],"prose_notes": "The observed data is a degenerate regime: B is so low that P(wrong majority) is negligible, so harness has nothing to fix but plenty to break. The decisive experiment must operate where B is high enough that wrong majorities are common but not so high that the correct answer is absent from the pool. Correlated errors are the key confound; the experiment must measure and report them."}
